name: CIMA MU12: La Barquilla Climb from Caravaca
name_original: Subida a La Barquilla y Fuentes del Marqués — vuelta desde Caravaca de la Cruz
description: Tackle the moderate CIMA MU12: La Barquilla Climb from Caravaca, a 30.4-mile road cycling route with 2941 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Caravaca De La Cruz traverse diverse landscapes, from the fertile plains of the Quípar and Argos rivers to pine forests and vineyards. The region features varied terrain, including well-developed cycling paths like the Noroeste Greenway, and offers significant elevation changes in its mountainous backdrops. This area provides a mix of paved and unpaved sections, catering to different cycling experiences. Best road cycling routes around Caravaca De La Cruz The most popular road cycling route is CIMA MU12: La Barquilla Climb from Caravaca, a 30.4 miles (49.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 33 minutes to complete. This route features a notable climb, offering a challenging ascent for road cyclists. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Moratalla – Argos Reservoir loop from Caravaca de la Cruz, a moderate 39.2 miles (63.1 km) path. This route offers scenic views around the Argos Reservoir and through varied terrain. Local road cyclists also love the Carretera Vieja de Archivel – Fuentes del Marqués loop from Caravaca de la Cruz, a 105.9 miles (170.4 km) trail leading through diverse landscapes including the Fuentes del Marqués Natural Park, often completed in about 8 hours 32 minutes. Road cycling around Caravaca De La Cruz is defined by greenways, river valleys, and mountainous areas. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to challenging climbs. The routes in Caravaca De La Cruz are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Caravaca De La Cruz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Caravaca de la Cruz?
A: There are over 35 road cycling routes around Caravaca de la Cruz, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a mix of moderate and challenging options, with a few easier routes also available.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Caravaca de la Cruz?
A: Yes, the region offers routes suitable for less experienced riders and families. The Noroeste Greenway (Vía Verde del Noroeste) is particularly well-suited for leisurely rides, traversing varied terrain like pine forests and vineyards. Additionally, the region research mentions a 14-kilometer circular route through iconic city landmarks and Las Fuentes del Marqués, which is suitable for families.

Q: What are some scenic highlights or natural attractions I can see while road cycling in the area?
A: Road cycling routes around Caravaca de la Cruz offer numerous scenic points. You can explore areas like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5133324" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Fuentes del Marqués Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, known for its abundant water sources and diverse flora. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5412052" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Argos Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is another beautiful spot, featured on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20125061" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moratalla – Argos Reservoir loop from Caravaca de la Cruz&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter the impressive gorge of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1589582" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estrecho de las Cuevas de la Encarnación&lt;/a&gt; or the refreshing &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4852802" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Somogil Baths&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes with significant climbs?
A: Absolutely. Caravaca de la Cruz is known for its mountainous backdrops, offering significant elevation changes for experienced riders. A notable challenging route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009242602/cima-mu12-la-barquilla-climb-from-caravaca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;CIMA MU12: La Barquilla Climb from Caravaca&lt;/a&gt;, which features a substantial ascent. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20127389" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carretera Vieja de Archivel – Fuentes del Marqués loop from Caravaca de la Cruz&lt;/a&gt;, a long trail with considerable elevation gain.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes around Caravaca de la Cruz?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20125061" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moratalla – Argos Reservoir loop from Caravaca de la Cruz&lt;/a&gt; and the extensive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20127389" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carretera Vieja de Archivel – Fuentes del Marqués loop from Caravaca de la Cruz&lt;/a&gt;. The region research also mentions a 14-kilometer circular route through city landmarks and Las Fuentes del Marqués.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Caravaca de la Cruz?
A: The road cycling routes in Caravaca de la Cruz are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 60 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from fertile plains to pine forests, and the mix of well-developed paths and challenging climbs.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by historical sites or castles?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. While cycling, you might pass by historical sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2746694" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Caravaca de la Cruz&lt;/a&gt;, which offers panoramic views, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5456197" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Templar Tower&lt;/a&gt; within Las Fuentes del Marqués. The Noroeste Greenway also showcases the area's cultural heritage as it connects charming towns.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Caravaca de la Cruz?
A: Caravaca de la Cruz offers good cycling conditions for much of the year. The region's climate generally allows for pleasant rides outside of the hottest summer months. Spring and autumn are particularly ideal, with milder temperatures and beautiful landscapes. Even winter can be suitable, especially for those seeking to escape colder climates, though specific route conditions should always be checked.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes for extended tours?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer rides, the region provides extensive options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20127389" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carretera Vieja de Archivel – Fuentes del Marqués loop from Caravaca de la Cruz&lt;/a&gt; is a significant route, stretching over 170 kilometers. The Noroeste Greenway also allows for multi-stage routes connecting various towns, offering opportunities for extended touring.

Q: Can I access road cycling routes using public transport in Caravaca de la Cruz?
A: While specific public transport connections directly to trailheads can vary, Caravaca de la Cruz itself is a well-connected town. The Noroeste Greenway, a key cycling path, connects Caravaca de la Cruz with other towns like Bullas, Cehegín, and Mula, which may offer public transport links. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for connections to these towns.

Q: Are there any routes that feature waterfalls or unique water features?
A: Yes, you can find routes that lead to interesting water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17267723" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Usero Waterfall – Salto del Usero loop from Caravaca de la Cruz&lt;/a&gt; is one such route. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4852802" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Somogil Baths&lt;/a&gt;, natural pools in the Sierra de Moratalla, offer a unique experience and can be incorporated into longer rides.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Caravaca de la Cruz?
A: The terrain around Caravaca de la Cruz is quite diverse. You'll encounter a mix of paved roads, suitable for road bikes, and sections of greenway which are largely on a gravel track. Routes traverse fertile plains, pine forests, vineyards, and mountainous areas, offering both flat stretches and significant climbs. This variety provides different cycling experiences for all preferences.
tips: Beautiful picture between trees that we find near the town of Caravaca de la Cruz while we roll along the Acequia del Sangrador.

Las Fuentes del Marqués is a natural space where there are several water springs surrounded by forest. It has grassy areas and picnic areas, as well as numerous paths for a walk. From here the routes start towards the tops of the Sierra del Gavilán.

At this point the ascent to La Barquilla or Alberquilla ends, which begins in Caravaca de la Cruz, a length of 8.2 km, a drop of 458 meters, an average slope of 5.58% and a maximum of 20%. One of those climbs that road bike fans in the Murcia region must do once in a lifetime.

faqs.question: Where does the CIMA MU12: La Barquilla Climb from Caravaca start?
faqs.answer: This challenging road cycling climb officially starts from Caravaca de la Cruz. You can begin your ascent from the town itself, heading towards the climb. Parking is generally available within Caravaca de la Cruz, a historic town with various public parking options.

faqs.question: What is the terrain and difficulty like for the La Barquilla Climb?
faqs.answer: The CIMA MU12: La Barquilla Climb is a demanding route, characterized by a consistent and significant gradient. It's 8.1 kilometers long with a vertical ascent of 523 meters, averaging 6.4%. The road surface is paved and suitable for racebikes, as is typical for CIMA climbs in Spain. It's rated as moderate difficulty on komoot, but the sustained effort required makes it a challenging ascent for most cyclists.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the La Barquilla Climb?
faqs.answer: As you gain elevation on the La Barquilla Climb, you'll experience increasingly panoramic views of the Murcian countryside and the historic town of Caravaca de la Cruz. The broader region features varied landscapes, from agricultural lands to arid pine forests and mountainous terrain, offering glimpses of surrounding ranges like Sierra del Gavilán and Pinar Negro.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the CIMA MU12: La Barquilla Climb from Caravaca?
faqs.answer: The full route, including the climb to La Barquilla and a return via Fuentes del Marqués, covers approximately 49 kilometers and typically takes around 2 hours and 30 minutes of active cycling. The climb itself is a significant portion of this duration due to its sustained gradient.

faqs.question: Are there any notable points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route features a few notable points. You'll pass along Carretera Vieja de Archivel, and the climb culminates at the La Alberquilla Summit. On the return, you'll also encounter Fuentes del Marqués, a natural monument.

faqs.question: What does the 'CIMA MU12' designation mean for this climb?
faqs.answer: CIMA (Centros de Interés de Montaña para Ascensión en Bicicleta) is a Spanish classification for significant and challenging cycling ascents. Its 'MU12' designation indicates it's the 12th such climb in the Murcia region, highlighting its importance and difficulty within the Spanish cycling community and making it a recognized goal for many road cyclists.

faqs.question: Is the CIMA MU12: La Barquilla Climb from Caravaca dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for road cycling. While dogs are generally not suitable companions for racebike climbs of this nature, if you were to visit the area for other activities, local regulations for dogs on trails would apply. For the climb itself, it's best to leave your furry friends at home.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this climb?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any specific permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle the CIMA MU12: La Barquilla Climb. It's a public road ascent, freely accessible to cyclists.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the La Barquilla Climb?
faqs.answer: The Murcia region generally offers good cycling weather for much of the year. Spring (March to May) and Autumn (September to November) are often ideal, providing milder temperatures and pleasant conditions for tackling challenging climbs like La Barquilla. Summers can be very hot, making early morning rides essential if cycling during those months.

faqs.question: Is this climb suitable for beginner road cyclists?
faqs.answer: Given its 8.1-kilometer length, 523 meters of elevation gain, and average gradient of 6.4%, this climb is quite demanding. While komoot rates the overall tour as moderate, the La Barquilla ascent itself requires sustained effort and a good level of fitness. It might be challenging for absolute beginner road cyclists, but those with some experience looking for a significant challenge will find it rewarding.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this road cycling climb?
faqs.answer: For this challenging road cycling climb, ensure you bring your racebike in good condition, appropriate cycling attire, a helmet, and plenty of water, especially during warmer months. Nutrition (energy bars, gels) is also recommended for sustained efforts. A basic repair kit (spare tube, pump, tire levers) is always wise, and a phone for emergencies.
